criteria.criterion: Clean preferred-schema responses continue to validate normally. — shipped: verify_findings, outcome_counts["clean"].
Recoverable non-empty responses from supported provider/model variations are normalized and accepted. — shipped: outcome_counts["recovered"].

Semantically empty verification results can never produce a valid pass or valid empty review. — shipped: decide.pass3_decide's no-verification → indeterminate rule covers both raw-empty and malformed-to-empty paths.

Empty-after-recovery and unrecoverable responses produce a distinct explicit indeterminate or contract-failure outcome. — shipped: outcome_counts["empty_outcomes"] / ["unrecoverable"], both routed to indeterminate by decide.pass3_decide.
Bounded retry/self-healing behavior remains provider-independent and deterministic at the orchestration boundary. — shipped: verify_findings's chunk/retry loop.

Telemetry separately counts clean parses, recovered parses, semantically empty outcomes, and unrecoverable responses. — shipped at the kernel-function level (verify_findings's outcome_counts); pipeline-level wiring into plan_review_decide and code_review_decide tracked as follow-up c2c5-53a7-2ce0-4d98.

Tests cover representative alternate wrappers/field shapes, successful recovery, empty normalization, retry exhaustion, and provider-version variation. — shipped in tests/unit/test_review_kernel.py / tests/unit/test_review_kernel_rules.py.
